title: rubus corylifolius subsp. balfourianus
intro: Rubus corylifolius subsp. balfourianus, commonly known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of raspberry native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Rubus corylifolius subsp. balfourianus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
